intro: Converting applications from C# to Ruby switches logic from a structured, enterprise-grade architecture (.NET) to an elegant, dynamically typed language optimized for developer happiness and object-oriented purity. Developers trade rigid compilation rules, high-performance static types, and strict interface validation for extreme object malleability, powerful runtime metaprogramming, and minimal syntax overhead. This migration maximizes scripting agility at the expense of raw computation velocity.

diffs.from: Static, nominal type structure checking code reliability strictly at compile time via explicit class signatures.
diffs.to: Dynamic, strong object type framework using duck-typing rules and resolving method evaluation completely at runtime.

diffs.from: Verbose brace layout requiring access block visibility specifications, semicolons, and extensive namespaces.
diffs.to: Clean, highly expressive human-readable structure using blocks instead of braces, omitting semicolons, and hiding parentheses.

diffs.from: Managed .NET generational memory allocation optimizing local stack allocations alongside tracked heap objects.
diffs.to: Automated runtime object tracing garbage collection tracking and sweeping dynamic object graphs automatically.

diffs.from: Advanced Task scheduler engine routing concurrent actions across multiple CPU core allocations.
diffs.to: Fiber-based and thread abstractions restricted historically by a Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) or utilizing async fiber loops.

diffs.from: Comprehensive corporate framework handling enterprise network layers, cryptographic streams, and LINQ transformations.
diffs.to: Rich, highly focused scripting core library emphasizing advanced text extraction, system scripting, and block iterations.

examples.title: Iterative Array Mapping Blocks
examples.description: Compares an inline C# lambda projection sequence with an idiomatic, clean Ruby enumerable block structure.

examples.title: Dynamic Dynamic Property Inspection
examples.description: Contrasts C# complex metadata reflection with simple, instantaneous method interrogation features inside Ruby.

tips.title: Everything is a Concrete Object
tips.body: In Ruby, numbers, primitive booleans, and nil values are not primitive types; they are full objects containing methods. You can call methods directly on raw numeric literals (e.g., `5.times`).

tips.title: Embrace Block and Yield Semantics
tips.body: Replace C# delegates and inline lambda parameters with native Ruby blocks. Use the `yield` keyword within methods to seamlessly pass custom execution logic down into functions.

tips.title: Utilize Built-in Duck Typing
tips.body: Stop building complex interface definitions just to verify type compliance. If a Ruby object implements the exact method you need, call it; the runtime resolves matching properties implicitly.

tips.title: Leverage Metaprogramming Intelligently
tips.body: Replace verbose C# reflection boilerplates with Ruby dynamic metaprogramming tools like `method_missing` or `define_method` to generate class functionalities dynamically at runtime.
